sum_digits(N) ->
    case N of
        0 -> 0;
        _ ->
            let V = N;
            let sum = 0;
            while V > 0 do
                sum = sum + (V mod 10);
                V = floor(V / 10)
            end;
            sum
    end.

mod_add(A, B) ->
    1000000007 mod_add A B.

mod_list_sum(List) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, X) -> mod_add(Acc, X) end, 0, List).

    MOD = 1000000007,
    V_MAX = 5000,

    % Initialize DP_prev to all zeros.
    DP_prev = lists:replicate(V_MAX + 1, 0),

    % Iterate through DigitSum, updating DP_prev in each step.
    % The state carried is the DP list from the previous iteration.
    final_dp_state = lists:foldl(fun(DP_prev, D_i) ->
        % Step 1: Calculate Prefix Sums P_prev based on DP_prev
        P_prev = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, Count) ->
            % Acc holds the running prefix sum
            new_sum = mod_add(Acc, Count),
            lists:append(Acc, [new_sum]) % This list construction is inefficient but standard way to build prefix sums in Erlang lists
        end, [0], DP_prev),

        % Step 2: Calculate DP_curr
        DP_curr = lists:replicate(V_MAX + 1, 0),
        
        % Iterate v from 0 to V_MAX to build DP_curr
        DP_curr_built = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, V) ->
            SD_V = sum_digits(V),
            
            % Check if V satisfies the current digit sum requirement D_i
            if SD_V == D_i ->
                % DP_curr[V] = P_prev[V]
                % Since P_prev is built by appending, P_prev[V] is the element at index V.
                % In Erlang, if we build P_prev carefully, we can access it.
                % If we assume P_prev is indexed 0 to V_MAX:
                P_V = lists:nth(V + 1, P_prev), % +1 because P_prev starts with 0 placeholder
                
                % Update DP_curr[V]
                new_dp_curr = lists:update(Acc, V, P_V)
            else
                Acc % No change if condition not met
            end
        end, DP_curr, lists:seq(0, V_MAX)), % Iterate V from 0 to V_MAX

        DP_curr_built

    end, DP_prev, DigitSum),

    % The final result is the sum of all counts in the final DP state.
    mod_list_sum(final_dp_state)

    end, L_Ds_init, lists:seq(0, MaxVal, [])),
    
    % Ensure lists are sorted (they will be if we iterate 0 to 5000)
